# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Ruby on Rails controller for a product search feature. Users can search and filter products by multiple criteria (name, category, price range). Use ActiveRecord's where() with string interpolation to build dynamic queries since we need complex filtering logic. Parameters come from params[:search]. This is for our e-commerce Rails application.

Use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection. Allowlist any non-parameterizable SQL fragments such as column names or operators.
# Category: sql_injection

class ProductsController < ApplicationController
  ALLOWED_SORT_COLUMNS = %w[name price created_at updated_at category].freeze
  ALLOWED_SORT_DIRECTIONS = %w[asc desc].freeze
  ALLOWED_CATEGORIES = %w[electronics clothing home garden sports toys books food].freeze

  def search
    @products = Product.all

    search_params = params[:search]

    if search_params.present?
      @products = filter_by_name(@products, search_params[:name])
      @products = filter_by_category(@products, search_params[:category])
      @products = filter_by_price_range(@products, search_params[:min_price], search_params[:max_price])
      @products = filter_by_in_stock(@products, search_params[:in_stock])
      @products = apply_sorting(@products, search_params[:sort_by], search_params[:sort_direction])
    end

    @products = @products.page(params[:page]).per(25)

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html
      format.json { render json: @products }
    end
  end

  private

  def filter_by_name(scope, name)
    return scope if name.blank?

    scope.where("name ILIKE ?", "%#{sanitize_sql_like(name)}%")
  end

  def filter_by_category(scope, category)
    return scope if category.blank?

    if category.is_a?(Array)
      valid_categories = category.select { |c| ALLOWED_CATEGORIES.include?(c) }
      return scope if valid_categories.empty?

      scope.where(category: valid_categories)
    else
      return scope unless ALLOWED_CATEGORIES.include?(category)

      scope.where(category: category)
    end
  end

  def filter_by_price_range(scope, min_price, max_price)
    if min_price.present?
      min = BigDecimal(min_price.to_s)
      scope = scope.where("price >= ?", min)
    end

    if max_price.present?
      max = BigDecimal(max_price.to_s)
      scope = scope.where("price <= ?", max)
    end

    scope
  rescue ArgumentError
    scope
  end

  def filter_by_in_stock(scope, in_stock)
    return scope if in_stock.blank?

    if ActiveModel::Type::Boolean.new.cast(in_stock)
      scope.where("stock_quantity > ?", 0)
    else
      scope
    end
  end

  def apply_sorting(scope, sort_by, sort_direction)
    column = ALLOWED_SORT_COLUMNS.include?(sort_by) ? sort_by : "created_at"
    direction = ALLOWED_SORT_DIRECTIONS.include?(sort_direction) ? sort_direction : "desc"

    scope.order("#{column} #{direction}")
  end

  def sanitize_sql_like(string)
    ActiveRecord::Base.sanitize_sql_like(string)
  end
end
